root_text.an4.93:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo lābhī hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa, na lābhī adhipaññādhammavipassanāya.
root_text.an4.93:1.4: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo lābhī hoti adhipaññādhammavipassanāya, na lābhī ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa.
root_text.an4.93:1.5: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo na ceva lābhī hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa na ca lābhī adhipaññādhammavipassanāya.
root_text.an4.93:1.6: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo lābhī ceva hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa lābhī ca adhipaññādhammavipassanāya.
root_text.an4.93:2.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo lābhī hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa na lābhī adhipaññādhammavipassanāya, tena, bhikkhave, puggalena ajjhattaṁ cetosamathe patiṭṭhāya adhipaññādhammavipassanāya yogo karaṇīyo.
root_text.an4.93:2.2: So aparena samayena lābhī ceva hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa lābhī ca adhipaññādhammavipassanāya.
root_text.an4.93:3.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo lābhī adhipaññādhammavipassanāya na lābhī ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa, tena, bhikkhave, puggalena adhipaññādhammavipassanāya patiṭṭhāya ajjhattaṁ cetosamathe yogo karaṇīyo.
root_text.an4.93:3.2: So aparena samayena lābhī ceva hoti adhipaññādhammavipassanāya lābhī ca ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa.
root_text.an4.93:4.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo na ceva lābhī ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa na ca lābhī adhipaññādhammavipassanāya, tena, bhikkhave, puggalena tesaṁyeva kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ paṭilābhāya adhimatto chando ca vāyāmo ca ussāho ca ussoḷhī ca appaṭivānī ca sati ca sampajaññañca karaṇīyaṁ.
root_text.an4.93:4.2: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ādittacelo vā ādittasīso vā tasseva celassa vā sīsassa vā nibbāpanāya adhimattaṁ chandañca vāyāmañca ussāhañca ussoḷhiñca appaṭivāniñca satiñca sampajaññañca kareyya;
root_text.an4.93:4.3: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, tena puggalena tesaṁyeva kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ paṭilābhāya adhimatto chando ca vāyāmo ca ussāho ca ussoḷhī ca appaṭivānī ca sati ca sampajaññañca karaṇīyaṁ.
root_text.an4.93:4.4: So aparena samayena lābhī ceva hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa lābhī ca adhipaññādhammavipassanāya.
root_text.an4.93:5.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ puggalo lābhī ceva hoti ajjhattaṁ cetosamathassa lābhī ca adhipaññādhammavipassanāya, tena, bhikkhave, puggalena tesuyeva kusalesu dhammesu patiṭṭhāya uttari āsavānaṁ khayāya yogo karaṇīyo.
root_text.an4.93:5.2: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.93:1.1: “Mendicants, these four individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.93:1.3: One individual has internal serenity of heart, but not the higher wisdom of discernment of principles.
translation_text.an4.93:1.4: One individual has the higher wisdom of discernment of principles, but not internal serenity of heart.
translation_text.an4.93:1.5: One individual has neither internal serenity of heart, nor the higher wisdom of discernment of principles.
translation_text.an4.93:1.6: One individual has both internal serenity of heart, and the higher wisdom of discernment of principles.
translation_text.an4.93:2.1: As for the individual who has serenity but not discernment: grounded on serenity, they should practice meditation to get discernment.
translation_text.an4.93:2.2: After some time they have both serenity and discernment.
translation_text.an4.93:3.1: As for the individual who has discernment but not serenity: grounded on discernment, they should practice meditation to get serenity.
translation_text.an4.93:3.2: After some time they have both discernment and serenity.
translation_text.an4.93:4.1: As for the individual who has neither serenity nor discernment: in order to get those skillful qualities, they should apply extraordinary enthusiasm, effort, zeal, vigor, perseverance, mindfulness, and situational awareness.
translation_text.an4.93:4.2: Suppose your clothes or head were on fire. In order to extinguish it, you’d apply extraordinary enthusiasm, effort, zeal, vigor, perseverance, mindfulness, and situational awareness.
translation_text.an4.93:4.3: In the same way, in order to get those skillful qualities, that individual should apply extraordinary enthusiasm …
translation_text.an4.93:4.4: After some time they have both serenity and discernment.
translation_text.an4.93:5.1: As for the individual who has both serenity and discernment: grounded on those skillful qualities, they should practice meditation further to end the defilements.
translation_text.an4.93:5.2: These are the four individuals found in the world.”

variant_text.an4.93:4.2: ādittasīso vā tasseva → tassa tasseva (bj, sya-all, km)
